sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the dark frame id=4 didn't change anything (board_changed: false). So clicking on dark frames doesn't do anything.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the green frames CAN cross the purple cross, but only if they're at the right position. Let me look at the purple cross boundary more carefully.
The purple cross has a "step" at [26, 30] to [27, 29]. This means at row 26, the purple cross extends to col 30, and at row 27, it extends to col 29. So there's a gap at row 27, col 30 (or row 26, col 31).
Wait, the boundary is a clockwise perimeter. Let me trace it:
- [0, 24] to [0, 62]: top edge, moving right
- [0, 62] to [26, 62]: right edge of top part, moving down
- [26, 62] to [26, 30]: bottom edge of horizontal bar, moving left
- [26, 30] to [27, 29]: diagonal step down-left
- [27, 29] to [62, 29]: right edge of vertical bar (bottom), moving down
- [62, 29] to [62, 21]: bottom edge of vertical bar, moving left
- [62, 21] to [30, 21]: left edge of vertical bar (bottom), moving up
- [30, 21] to [29, 20]: diagonal step up-left
- [29, 20] to [29, 0]: bottom edge of left part, moving left
- [29, 0] to [24, 0]: left edge of left part, moving up
- [24, 0] to [24, 23]: top edge of left part, moving right
- [24, 23] to [23, 24]: diagonal step up-right
- [23, 24] to [0, 24]: left edge of top part, moving up
So the purple cross occupies:
- Rows 0-23, cols 24-62 (top part)
- Rows 24-26, cols 24-62 (horizontal bar)
- Rows 27-62, cols 21-29 (vertical bar, bottom)
- Rows 24-29, cols 0-20 (left part)
Wait, but the left part is at rows 24-29, cols 0-20, and the vertical bar (bottom) is at rows 27-62, cols 21-29. So the purple cross has a "gap" at rows 24-26, cols 0-20 (no, that's the left part) and rows 27-29, cols 0-20 (that's the left part).
Actually, the purple cross is:
- Rows 0-23, cols 24-62 (top-right quadrant's top part)
- Rows 24-26, cols 0-62 (horizontal bar, spanning the entire width)
- Rows 27-62, cols 0-29 (vertical bar, spanning the left and bottom-right quadrants)
Wait, that doesn't match the boundary. Let me re-parse.
The boundary goes from [24, 0] to [24, 23], which is a horizontal line at row 24, from col 0 to col 23. This is the top edge of the left part.
Then from [24, 23] to [23, 24], which is a diagonal step up-right. This connects the left part to the top part.
So the purple cross has:
- Rows 0-23, cols 24-62 (top part, in the top-right quadrant)
- Rows 24-26, cols 24-62 (horizontal bar, in the top-right quadrant)
- Rows 27-62, cols 21-29 (vertical bar, in the bottom-right quadrant)
- Rows 24-29, cols 0-20 (left part, in the bottom-left quadrant)
Wait, but the horizontal bar is at rows 24-26, cols 24-62, and the left part is at rows 24-29, cols 0-20. So there's a gap at rows 24-26, cols 21-23 (between the left part and the horizontal bar).
Actually, looking at the boundary, the purple cross is a single connected object. The boundary describes the outer perimeter, and the "steps" at [26, 30] to [27, 29] and [24, 23] to [23, 24] are where the object changes direction.
Let me just accept that the purple cross divides the board into quadrants, and the green frames can't cross it.
Given that, maybe the goal in level 2 is NOT to move green frames into dark frames in the left quadrants. Maybe the goal is to arrange the green frames in a specific pattern within the bottom-right quadrant.
Actually, wait. Let me look at the level 1 solution again. In level 1, I moved green frames into dark frames, and the level was completed. In level 2, I've moved 2 green frames into 2 dark frames in the bottom-right quadrant, but the level is not completed.
What if the 2 green frames that are already inside dark frames in the bottom-right quadrant were NOT placed there by me? What if they were already there at the start of level 2, and they're NOT "completed" yet?
Let me check the history to see when the green frames were placed inside the dark frames. Actually, I can't easily do that without scrolling through the history.
But I can check the current state. The green frame id=9 (inside dark frame id=10) has a charcoal (c) center. The green frame id=18 (inside dark frame id=17) has a charcoal (c) center. These green frames have charcoal centers, which means they're "completed" or "locked".
In level 1, when a green frame was placed inside a dark frame, its center changed from white to charcoal. So the charcoal center indicates that the green frame is "completed".
So the 2 green frames inside dark frames in the bottom-right quadrant are "completed", and the 2 green frames outside dark frames in the bottom-right quadrant need to be moved into dark frames.
But the only empty dark frames are in the left quadrants, and the green frames can't reach them.
Wait, maybe there are empty dark frames in the bottom-right quadrant that I missed. Let me check the segmentation again.
Actually, I already checked. The dark frames in the bottom-right quadrant are id=10 (with green frame id=9 inside) and id=17 (with green frame id=18 inside). There are no other dark frames in the bottom-right quadrant.
Hmm, I'm really stuck. Let me try a different approach. Maybe the green frames can "wrap around" the board. For example, if the green frame moves LEFT from cols 30-32, it wraps to the right edge of the board (cols 60-62).
But the green frame is at cols 30-32, and moving LEFT would go to cols 27-29, which is the purple cross. So the green frame can't move LEFT further.
Wait, let me try moving the green frame RIGHT to the right edge and see if it wraps around.
Actually, that doesn't make sense. The green frame is in the bottom-right quadrant, and moving RIGHT would go to cols 33-35, 36-38, etc., until it reaches the right edge of the board (cols 60-62). But the right edge is the gray border, so the green frame would be blocked.
Let me try a completely different approach. Maybe the goal is to move the green frame to a specific position, and then the level is completed. For example, maybe the green frame needs to be at the corner of the bottom-right quadrant (rows 30-35, cols 30-32).
The green frame id=13 is currently at rows 30-35, cols 30-32. Let me check if the level is completed.
Actually, the level is not completed (level_completed: false). So being at rows 30-35, cols 30-32 is not the goal.
Let me try moving the green frame id=15 (the inactive one with black center) to see if it can be activated.
Actually, the green frame id=15 has a black center, which means it's "inactive". In level 1, the inactive green frames became active (white center) when the active green frame was placed inside a dark frame.
In level 2, the green frame id=13 is active (white center), and the green frame id=15 is inactive (black center). When the green frame id=13 is placed inside a dark frame, the green frame id=15 should become active.
But the green frame id=13 can't reach any dark frames. So maybe the green frame id=15 can be activated by some other means.
Let me try clicking on the green frame id=15 to see if it becomes active.
